name: Ilya Sutskever's Reading List
description: Papers recommended by Ilya Sutskever, co-founder of OpenAI. Covers foundational ML, transformers, scaling, information theory, complexity, and AGI theory.

papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.abstract: Introduces the Transformer architecture, relying entirely on self-attention mechanisms without recurrence or convolutions.

papers.title: Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
papers.abstract: Introduces the attention mechanism for neural machine translation, allowing models to focus on relevant parts of the input.

papers.title: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of Recurrent Neural Networks
papers.abstract: Blog post demonstrating the surprising power of RNNs for character-level language modeling across various domains.

papers.title: Understanding LSTM Networks
papers.abstract: Visual explanation of LSTM architecture and how it solves the vanishing gradient problem in RNNs.

papers.title: Recurrent Neural Network Regularization
papers.abstract: Presents dropout techniques specifically designed for RNNs to prevent overfitting.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.abstract: Empirical study showing power-law relationships between model performance and size, data, and compute.

papers.title: GPipe: Easy Scaling with Micro-Batch Pipeline Parallelism
papers.abstract: Introduces pipeline parallelism for training very large neural networks across multiple accelerators.

papers.title: ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
papers.abstract: The AlexNet paper that sparked the deep learning revolution by winning ImageNet 2012.

papers.title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
papers.abstract: Introduces residual connections enabling training of very deep networks (100+ layers).

papers.title: Identity Mappings in Deep Residual Networks
papers.abstract: Analyzes residual networks and proposes improved designs for better gradient flow.

papers.title: Multi-Scale Context Aggregation by Dilated Convolutions
papers.abstract: Introduces dilated/atrous convolutions for dense prediction without losing resolution.

papers.title: Pointer Networks
papers.abstract: Neural architecture that learns to point to elements in the input sequence for variable-length outputs.

papers.title: Order Matters: Sequence to Sequence for Sets
papers.abstract: Explores how input/output ordering affects seq2seq models when dealing with sets.

papers.title: Neural Turing Machines
papers.abstract: Combines neural networks with external memory for learning algorithmic tasks.

papers.title: A Simple Neural Network Module for Relational Reasoning
papers.abstract: Introduces Relation Networks for learning to reason about relationships between objects.

papers.title: Relational Recurrent Neural Networks
papers.abstract: Extends RNNs with relational memory cores using multi-head attention.

papers.title: Neural Message Passing for Quantum Chemistry
papers.abstract: Unifying framework for graph neural networks applied to molecular property prediction.

papers.title: Variational Lossy Autoencoder
papers.abstract: Combines VAEs with autoregressive models for controllable representation learning.

papers.title: Keeping Neural Networks Simple by Minimizing the Description Length of the Weights
papers.abstract: Foundational paper on using MDL principle for neural network regularization.

papers.title: Deep Speech 2: End-to-End Speech Recognition in English and Mandarin
papers.abstract: End-to-end deep learning approach to speech recognition across languages.

papers.title: The First Law of Complexodynamics
papers.abstract: Explores why complexity rises then falls in closed systems, unlike entropy.

papers.title: Quantifying the Rise and Fall of Complexity in Closed Systems: The Coffee Automaton
papers.abstract: Empirical study of complexity dynamics using cellular automata.

papers.title: A Tutorial Introduction to the Minimum Description Length Principle
papers.abstract: Comprehensive introduction to MDL for model selection and inference.

papers.title: Machine Super Intelligence
papers.abstract: PhD thesis on formal measures of machine intelligence and paths to superintelligence.

papers.title: Dense Passage Retrieval for Open-Domain Question Answering
papers.abstract: Introduces DPR for dense retrieval outperforming sparse methods like BM25.

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
papers.abstract: Combines retrieval with generation for knowledge-grounded language models.

papers.title: Zephyr: Direct Distillation of LM Alignment
papers.abstract: Distilled DPO approach for aligning small LMs without human feedback.

papers.title: Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts
papers.abstract: Reveals U-shaped attention pattern where LLMs struggle with middle context.

papers.title: Better & Faster Large Language Models via Multi-token Prediction
papers.abstract: Training LLMs to predict multiple tokens simultaneously for efficiency.
